theme: Voices & Vitals — Give Every Character a Personality and Every Surface a Pulse

ideas.title: Vibe-Reactive Boss Honorific in Header Greeting
ideas.description: The header greeting always says 'boss' regardless of team state. Replace the hardcoded 'boss' span with a vibe-reactive lookup: crushing → 'LEGEND 🔥', on-fire → 'captain 🚨', in-flow → 'boss', slow-day → 'chief... you still there? 😐', after-hours → 'night owl 🌙'. This is 5 lines of code — a const object and a lookup — for a moment of delight on every single page load. The greeting is the FIRST thing visitors read. WHY: It's the cheapest personalization this app can do. During a crushing sprint, being greeted as 'LEGEND' hits completely differently.

ideas.title: Vibe-Reactive Floor Grid Glow
ideas.description: The office floor grid lines are always the same dim color regardless of team state. The floor takes up 70% of the screen but stays completely static. Add data-vibe={vibe} to .office-floor and drive --grid-color CSS variable per vibe: crushing → subtle violet pulse (rgba(139,92,246,0.18)), on-fire → warm red-orange glow, in-flow → cool teal, slow-day → nearly invisible grey, after-hours → moonlight blue. The grid is already rendered via background-image in Office.css — just override the color variable. WHY: The vibe pill reacts but the largest visual element doesn't. When the team is crushing it, the ROOM should glow.

ideas.title: Session Duration Self-Roast in Footer
ideas.description: The footer says 'v1.0.0-chaos'. Add a live session timer next to it using mountTimeRef = useRef(Date.now()) updated by the existing useTimeTick. Commentary escalates: 0-29m → plain 'Session: Nm'; 30-59m → 'Session: Nm 🫠'; 60-119m → 'Session: 1h Nm — maybe get some water.'; 120m+ → 'Session: Nh Nm — Bloberto is worried about you.' WHY: Pure Bloberto humor. The app already runs on mild existential dread — the version is 'v1.0.0-chaos'. A session timer that roasts you after an hour is perfectly on brand. Zero infrastructure cost, infinite personality dividend.

ideas.title: New Hire Entrance Door Walk-In Animation
ideas.description: The entrance door exists at the bottom of the office floor. When polling detects a first-time worker (the firstTime array in App.jsx already computed), that worker should appear at the entrance position (~50%, 88%) and animate to their desk over 1.5s via CSS transition. In Office.jsx, accept a newArrivals prop (Set of worker IDs). Characters in that set start at entrance coordinates with a 1.5s transition to desk position. WHY: The activity log says '🟢 Maya joined' but the floor just... has Maya at her desk. The door exists. The hire event is tracked. Watching someone walk through the front door is a completely different emotional experience than a tile appearing.

ideas.title: Bloberto's Manager Desk Live Clock + Sync Countdown
ideas.description: Bloberto's manager desk has a monitor element and nameplate but shows no live data. Add a small HH:MM clock (updated every minute via useEffect) and a sync countdown '⟳ 28s' (derived from POLL_INTERVAL_ACTIVE - timeSinceLastSync). Style as monospace LCD-aesthetic inside the desk box. WHY: The manager desk is the spatial command center but shows only static content. A ticking clock and countdown turn it into a live readout — you feel the polling rhythm. Also stops impatient users from hammering the sync button.

ideas.title: Keyboard Shortcut Cheat Sheet Modal
ideas.description: The app already has keyboard shortcuts (1=Office, 2=Dashboard, R=Refresh, Esc=blur) but no discoverability. Add a '?' shortcut that shows a small modal overlay listing all shortcuts. Dismiss with Esc or clicking outside. Style it as a floating card with the same dark theme. WHY: Power users never find hidden shortcuts. A simple '?' modal is standard UX (GitHub, Gmail all do this). One component, zero backend.

ideas.title: The Entrance Door Has Opinions — Arrival/Departure Reactions
ideas.description: The entrance door is pure scenery. Make it reactive: (a) On hire/arrival, door glows green with '👋 Welcome!' tooltip for 2s via CSS @keyframes pulse; (b) on worker disappearing (the disappeared array in App.jsx), door pulses orange '👋 See ya!' for 2s; (c) during after-hours with 0 workers, add a '🔒 CLOSED' badge. Pass doorEvent prop to Office.jsx, reset to null after 2000ms. WHY: Every office has a front door that witnesses comings and goings. Making it react to arrivals and departures creates a spatial metaphor no data panel can replicate.
